data.title: Skeena Gold & Silver proposes $750M senior secured notes offering
data.description:
• Skeena Gold & Silver (SKE) announced on Tuesday that it plans to offer $750M in senior secured notes due 2031, to be guaranteed by certain subsidiaries and secured by a first-priority lien on assets, including interests in the Eskay Creek project.

  • Under an agreement with stream purchasers, it plans to make a lump-sum payment of about $184 million to reduce the stream percentage deliverable from the Eskay Creek project by about 66.67%.
  • The company plans to use about $184M for the stream buy-down, $100M for an interest reserve, and the remainder for the Eskay Creek project, fees, and general corporate purposes.
  • Additionally, it plans to cancel its existing $350M  senior secured term loan and cost overrun facility alongside the offering and stream buy-down, noting the facilities are undrawn and no fees are expected, subject to successful completion of the transactions.
  • The offering and related refinancing are expected to improve operating margins, increase exposure to gold prices and production, and enhance overall economics of the Eskay Creek project.

data.title: Hershey has a salty plan to boost sales
data.description:
The Hershey Company (HSY) is holding its 2026 Investor Day on Tuesday at the New York Stock Exchange. The management team plans to outline Hershey's strategic priorities and financial framework for the near and long term.

A key part of the presentation will focus on what the company calls "next-generation portfolio and innovation." The plan is to build on iconic confection brands to expand into premium and better-for-you options while accelerating growth in salty and functional snacking categories. Hershey (HSY) said it has a robust five-year innovation pipeline to grow the core and expand into the fastest-growing parts of snacking.

"With a differentiated portfolio, we are uniquely positioned to win with our iconic core brands and expand faster in high-growth spaces. Through One Hershey, we go to market as one integrated team across sweet, salty, and functional snacking. The strategy is clear. The team is ready. The next chapter of growth and leading performance starts now," updated CEO Kirk Tanner ahead of the event.

Hershey (HSY) provided a guidance update ahead of its presentation. For FY26, the food giant expects net sales growth of 4% to 5%, organic net sales growth of 2.5% to 3.5%, reported earnings per share growth of 79% to 89%, and adjusted earnings per share growth of 30% to 35%.

Shares of Hershey (HSY) edged 0.6% higher in premarket trading.

data.title: Micron's price target set lower by Citi after recent pullback in DRAM spot prices
data.description:
Mainstream DDR5 16GB DRAM product prices have declined 6% since Micron Technology (MU) reported its second-quarter earnings on March 18, prompting Citi to reduce its price target on the stock to $425 from $510.

"That said, Micron and its memory peers have begun negotiations with the hyperscalers on 3-5 year strategic or long-term agreements to lock in base volumes, prepayments, and adjustments to quarterly pricing based on market conditions, which should provide support to contract prices, in our view," said Citi analysts in a Tuesday investor note.

Citi also indicated that Google's (GOOG)(GOOGL) TurboQuant buzz has led to the lower spot price on DRAM.

"Citi expects TurboQuant to have similar effects to DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK)," the financial firm noted. "At face value, a new efficiency technique such as TurboQuant reduces AI compute and memory cost per inquiry, but it also further unlocks usage, which ultimately increases compute/memory demand. We see the computing demand as a function of (1) individual computing needs and (2) usage. Demand for compute/memory shrinking will depend on the differential between how much (1) is coming down via innovations and (2) is going up as a factor of lower computing cost. Historically, we think cheaper technology has mostly increased the demand for more technology. We see AI as no different."

Since Google revealed TurboQuant in a blog post on March 24, Micron's share price has declined by 19%. The decline has spread across memory and storage stocks, including Western Digital (WDC), Seagate Technology (STX), and Sandisk (SNDK).

"TurboQuant, as described in the Google Research Blog in detail, is a compression technique that promises model size reduction via new quantization techniques," Citi noted. "This eases KV cache and vector search calculations. In a nutshell, TurboQuant first randomly rotates the data vectors to find key marking via the PolarQuant technique to simplify the vector to better summarize it via a tool called Quantizer. Second, TurboQuant efficiently utilizes another tool called QJL algorithm to eliminate the small number of errors coming from the quantization phase."

data.title: The consumer cost of war
data.description:
U.S. consumers are increasingly feeling the Iran war when they pull up to the gas station.

Gasoline prices (UNG) in the U.S. this week surged past $4 a gallon for the first time since August 2022, marking one of the most visible economic consequences of the escalating conflict in the Middle East.

The nationwide average retail price for regular unleaded gasoline reached $4.018 a gallon on Monday, according to the American Automobile Association. Prices have climbed more than $1 since hostilities began, rising from $2.98 the day before the U.S. and Israel launched attacks against Iran.

Even after adjusting for wages, pump prices are jumping back toward $4 in February 2026 dollars, according to Augur Infinity . That is still trailing the worst affordability hits of 2008 and the early 2010s, though the speed of this move is more telling. An AAA chart, meanwhile, shows 2026 pump prices surging well above the usual seasonal path, underscoring the magnitude of the war's consumer impact.

At the same time, U.S. WTI crude oil prices (CL1:COM) settled above $100 a barrel for the first time since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine in 2022. The gains complicate the Federal Reserve’s efforts to manage inflation while maintaining employment levels. Research from Ryan Cummings and Neale Mahoney at Stanford University’s Institute for Economic Policy Research finds that consumer sentiment, as measured by the University of Michigan survey, slides 4.5 points or more, even after accounting for other impacts.

data.title: VolitionRx Limited secures €2M non-dilutive funding from Belgian agencies
data.description:
• VolitionRx Limited (NYSE AMERICAN: VNRX) on Tuesday said that it secured €2.0 million in non-dilutive financing from Namur Invest and Wallonie Entreprendre.
  • The company said it will use the proceeds to support development of its Nu.Q product portfolio, including regulatory transition work for Nu.Q NETs, as well as ongoing research and development of a lateral flow product aimed at improving access in low-income countries.
  • Volition said the funding forms part of its strategy to secure non-dilutive financing to support continued development, adding that total non-dilutive funding to date exceeds $25 million.
  • VNRX +0.05% premarket to $0.1901.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cisco, Arista Networks among Truist's favorite networking stocks
data.description:
• Cisco Systems (CSCO), Arista Networks (ANET), and Motorola Solutions (MSI) are Truist's favorite networking stocks, as the investment firm sees the trio benefiting from artificial intelligence spending.
  • “Networking/Hardware represents the group most directly linked to elevated AI/cloud investment, at a time when US hyperscaler capex alone is forecasted to total ~$700bn in 2026E, up nearly 60% yoy (following +66% and +55% the last two years!),” analyst Matthew Niknam wrote in a note to clients.
  • Niknam added that looking beyond just the AI spending, the transition to inference from training has resulted in more spending in areas such as traditional servers and campus networking. 
  • “That said, we also favor a more selective approach to the stocks, considering
    relatively higher valuations (at/near decade+ highs, in select cases), and our
    expectation for more inflationary headwinds (memory, shipping/freight, on top of
    existing tariff impacts) that could impact volumes/demand in forward periods,” Niknam added.
  • Niknam has a Buy rating on Cisco, Arista, and Motorola Solutions, and his price targets are $94, $161, and $540, respectively.

data.title: Tantech receives Nasdaq non-compliance notice
data.description:
• Tantech Holdings (TANH) received a Nasdaq notice for failing the $1 minimum bid price requirement over 30 consecutive days.
  • The company has 180 days, until September 22, 2026, to regain compliance.
  • Compliance can be achieved if shares trade at or above $1 for at least 10 consecutive business days.
  • Tantech may use measures like a reverse stock split to meet the requirement.
  • If unmet, the company could qualify for an additional 180-day grace period or face potential delisting.

data.title: Mountain Valley plans C$2M private placement, debt settlement transactions
data.description:
• Mountain Valley (CSE: MVMD) (OTC: MVMDF) on Monday said that it intends to raise up to CAD $2 million through a non-brokered private placement of units priced at CDN $0.025 each.
  • Each unit will include one common share and one warrant exercisable at CDN $0.08 per share for 12 months, with potential acceleration if certain price conditions are met.
  • The offering is expected to close in April 2026, subject to regulatory approvals.
  • Separately, the company plans to settle CDN $485,000 in debt through two shares-for-debt transactions.
  • One tranche will cover CAD $385,000 at CDN $0.017 per share, while the second will settle CAD $100,000 at CDN $0.06 per share.
  • The company said the transactions are aimed at substantially eliminating outstanding debt and strengthening its balance sheet.
  • MVMDF closed +16.59% at $0.0548.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Fosun International reports FY results
data.description:
• Fosun International press release (FOSUF): FY Group's total revenue reached RMB173.43B.
  • Adjusted industrial operation profit amounted to RMB 4B.
  • The four core subsidiaries generated RMB128.2B in revenue, accounting for 74% of the Group's total revenue.

data.title: Conagra Brands declares $0.35 dividend
data.description:
• Conagra Brands (CAG) declares $0.35/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 8.9%
  • Payable June 3; for shareholders of record April 30; ex-div April 30.
  • See CAG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
